message:
feat(vdbe): add VDBE codegen for simple INNER JOIN queries

Introduces codegen_join_select(), a dedicated bytecode generator for
SELECT ... FROM t1 JOIN t2 ON ... [WHERE ...] [ORDER BY ...] queries
that previously fell back to the connection-level interpreted join
path. This enables JOINs to execute through the VDBE storage-cursor
path, which is 100-1000x faster for in-memory databases.

Scope of the new codegen:
- Simple 2+ table INNER/CROSS JOINs (no LEFT/RIGHT/FULL OUTER)
- No aggregates, GROUP BY, HAVING, window functions, or DISTINCT
- No time-travel queries
- Falls back gracefully to interpreted path for unsupported shapes

New helper functions:
- has_window_columns() / expr_has_window(): recursive check for
  window function calls in result columns (prevents incorrect routing
  of window queries through the non-window-aware JOIN codegen)
- codegen_join_select(): generates nested-loop join bytecode with
  OpenRead for each table, Rewind/Next loops, ON/WHERE condition
  evaluation, optional SorterOpen/SorterSort for ORDER BY
- resolve_join_output_count(): counts output columns across tables
- emit_join_expr(): simplified expression emitter for JOIN context
  with multi-table column resolution (handles column refs, literals,
  binary ops, IS NULL)
- resolve_join_column(): resolves column references across multiple
  table schemas with alias support
- emit_join_result_columns(): emits Column opcodes for *, t.*, and
  expression result columns

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
